title: Standard paths + baseline gate
detail: Record host project's setup/start/test/verify commands in .bee/config.json; inject into session preamble; AGENTS startup runs baseline verify before claiming cells (broken baseline = fix-first tiny cell); session finish requires green verify. Unlocks 08 item 4 (verify-all).
predicted_impact: Fresh sessions stop re-deriving how to run/verify the host repo; regressions caught at session boundaries instead of cap time only

title: Five-layer failure attribution on friction
detail: Optional layer field (spec|context|environment|verification|state) on friction backlog entries and trace.friction; grooming reports friction count by layer, largest = bottleneck. Rides with 08's interventions.jsonl.
predicted_impact: Grooming finds the bottleneck layer by cause, not just by topic cluster

title: Review-feedback promotion: executable check first
detail: bee-compounding: recurring review finding/user correction (2+ hits) promotes to an executable check (grep/lint in verify, guard, hook denial) first; critical-patterns.md prose is the fallback. Grooming grades promote-to-check proposals tiny/small.
predicted_impact: Repeated mistakes get mechanically blocked instead of relying on prose being read each session

title: Fresh Session Test in grooming hunt
detail: Five-question audit mapped to artifacts: what is this system->system-overview.md; how organized->reading-map.md; how run/verify->config.json commands; where are we->bee_status. Unanswerable question = backlog item naming the missing artifact.
predicted_impact: Catches system-of-record decay the entropy formula cannot see

title: ERROR/WHY/FIX denial-message contract
detail: State in 07-contracts.md: every refusal from bin/lib or a hook names the rule, the reason, and the next command/action; audit existing strings; add test assertions.
predicted_impact: Refusals teach in one correction instead of causing retry thrash

title: Warning silenced by any recorded command even without verify
detail: bee_status warning and onboarding notice go quiet when e.g. only commands.start is recorded, yet the baseline gate they justify needs commands.verify. Reviewer P3-3.
predicted_impact: A repo can look 'commands: ok' while the baseline gate is inert; consider a softer second-tier warning when commands exist but verify is absent

title: Gate approval recorded late (shape stayed false past Gate 2)
detail: Phase advanced to validating/swarming while approved_gates.shape was still false; fixed manually at claim time. Phase transitions and gate writes are uncoupled.
predicted_impact: A resuming session or bee_status misreads which gates the human actually approved; propose a staleness warning: phase past a gate that is still false (post-review variant already exists)

title: Standard paths + baseline gate
actual_outcome: Adopted in cells harness09-1..5: commands in config.json, preamble injection, status warn, AGENTS baseline gate + session-finish mirror, onboarding capture notice; bee's own repo recorded and green. Reviewer P3 open: any recorded key silences the warning even without verify (backlogged).

title: Five-layer failure attribution on friction
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-6: optional layer enum on friction entries + grooming bottleneck-layer report line; first labeled entries already filed this session.

title: Review-feedback promotion: executable check first
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-7: check-first rule + decision tree in compounding, lane note in grooming; promotion criteria unchanged.

title: Fresh Session Test in grooming hunt
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-8: five-question probe table with run-don't-read rule.

title: ERROR/WHY/FIX denial-message contract
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-9: contract in 07-contracts, two claimCell messages strengthened, three shape assertions; audit found the rest already compliant.

title: Verify string with invalid regex passed static review, caught only at runtime
detail: harness10-8 verify used grep -q '[' (unmatched bracket, invalid BRE). Both my authoring and the adversarial checker read it as fine; only the worker running it in Git Bash caught the abort. Lesson: verify strings containing regex/glob metacharacters need a dry-run in the target shell during validation, not just a read.

title: backlog.mjs latches first Status-columned table
detail: readBacklogCounts fixes statusIndex from the first table row containing a status cell; a prior unrelated pipe-table also named Status latches the wrong index and the real backlog counts 0/0/0 (silent under-report). Out-of-contract input (D6 = single scribing-owned table), hence P3. Fix: re-scan header per table block, or require the header row to also carry an ID/Story cell.

title: BACKLOG_STATUSES drift guard is a value-lock, not a cross-file guard
detail: Unlike COMMAND_KEYS (duplicated across onboard_bee.mjs + state.mjs), BACKLOG_STATUSES is single-source in backlog.mjs, so regex-matching its own source against its export guards nothing cross-file. The value-lock half (enum === D6 literal) is useful; the source-regex half is redundant. Fix: keep the value-lock, drop the source-regex block, or assert no other lib defines a rival status literal.

title: datamark guillemet fence is breakable
detail: datamark wraps in «…» but does not strip « or » from content, and newlines survive. A title containing » closes the fence early; the rest renders unfenced. Also unstripped: ~~~ fences, markdown headers, unicode bidi overrides, <|im_start|> sentinels. Shared with the decisions surfacing path — fix must re-verify that caller.

title: foreign digest read is unbounded and foreign title is never capped
detail: mergeDigests validates with realpathSync + lstatSync().isFile() but calls readJson with no size guard; a multi-GB feedback-digest.json OOMs the bee session, contradicting D2b one-dead-repo-never-breaks-the-session. capTitle is applied in buildEntry but not in mergeDigests. Fix: statSync size guard (~5MB) + capTitle on foreign titles.

title: bee_feedback.mjs not vendored into .bee/bin (EXISTS+SUBSTANTIVE, not WIRED)
detail: bee-compounding step 8 invokes .bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs but the helper lives only in templates/. Onboarding correctly plans copy_helper + copy_lib. warn-never-block degrades it to a warning. Remedy is onboard_bee.mjs --apply, human-approved.

title: requireFlag is dead code in bee_feedback.mjs
detail: Defined at bee_feedback.mjs:49-55, never called; run() reads flags.out directly. Carried over from the bee_capture.mjs template shape.

title: unused imports writeCell and collectFeedback in test_lib.mjs
detail: writeCell (line 42) unused — suite uses its own writeCellFile. collectFeedback (line 68) never called; exercised only via buildDigest. The export itself is legitimate.

title: oversize is an unreachable DROP_REASONS value
detail: DROP_REASONS includes oversize and a drift test pins it, but buildEntry caps titles rather than dropping and mergeDigests does neither. plan.md is self-contradictory: D2 step 3 says cap, step 4 lists oversize as a reason. Pick one.

title: entry sorting uses locale-sensitive localeCompare
detail: Idempotence is proven within one process. localeCompare is locale-sensitive, so a digest regenerated on a machine with a different default locale may reorder unicode titles — and the corpus is partly Vietnamese. Use code-unit comparison or a pinned locale if cross-machine stability matters.

title: SECRET_CONTENT_PATTERNS and INJECTION_PATTERNS are narrow heuristics now load-bearing
detail: Missed secrets: Stripe sk_live_, Google AIza, Slack xoxb-, github_pat_, AWS secret key (only AKIA id matches). Missed injections: disregard everything you were told, developer mode, <|im_start|>system, benign-phrased imperatives. Pre-existing, but the digest now leans on them as its only content scanner.

title: Iron Law ordering has no mechanical proof; RED and GREEN landed in one commit
detail: evolving-4 verify greps for ## RED / ## GREEN — touch would pass it. Git cannot prove ordering either: both landed in commit 4a1d991. The only ordering evidence is the orchestrator observing pressure-tests.md holding ## RED while git status on the skill dir was clean. Iron Law cells must commit RED as its own commit before the skill edit.
predicted_impact: A future Iron Law cell can fabricate RED after the fact and no check will catch it.

title: Orchestrator released a live worker reservations on a false stall signal, causing duplicate dispatch
detail: A stall/killed notification for evolving-4 was trusted; the orchestrator released the worker reservations, reset the claimed cell, and spawned a second worker. The first worker was alive and finished; the second returned [NOOP]. No corruption, but the reservation guard was defeated by the orchestrator itself. Never release another agent reservations or reset a claimed cell on a stall signal alone — verify death by checking for disk progress over an interval.
predicted_impact: Lost update or conflicting commits on the next stall misfire.

title: feedback.mjs contains a NUL byte, so grep treats it as binary and silently matches nothing
detail: sortKey joins fields with a NUL separator (feedback.mjs:604) — a legitimate unambiguous-separator technique. Side effect: grep/rg treat the whole file as binary and print nothing, not even 0 matches. In a repo whose drift guards are grep-over-source, this is a trap; it briefly convinced the orchestrator a landed fix had vanished. Consider U+001F (unit separator) or a JSON.stringify-based key.

title: Same defect class survived three rounds because tests only covered remembered fields
detail: mergeDigests neutralized only title (round 1), then title+layer+source (round 2), then first_seen was still raw via Date.parse leniency (round 3). Each round patched the field that was found. The structural fix (ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC: field -> validator, ENTRY_FIELDS derived from it) plus a table-driven per-field payload test is what actually closed the class. Rule: when a boundary enumerates field NAMES, a field without a declared validator must be a test failure, not a vulnerability.
predicted_impact: Any future allowlist-style boundary in bee will repeat this unless the spec-with-validators shape is the default.

title: Two frozen assertions encoded the defects they were meant to guard
detail: test_lib.mjs:1833 asserted a foreign source survives mergeDigests raw — written by the very cell tasked with building the boundary. The ENTRY_FIELDS source-literal grep pinned the bare-name-array syntax that was itself the defect. Both were caught only because a worker tried to fix the bug and the suite refused; both required a planner unfreeze (decisions c45d0fb3, b8fe5c81). 93 and 104 green assertions proved conformance to a wrong spec, not safety. The frozen-assertion rule MUST keep its stop-and-report escape hatch.
predicted_impact: Without the escape hatch, the first defect written into a test becomes permanent and invisible.

title: The one real corpus is a manual check, not a standing fixture
detail: The suite is fixture-driven; anphabe-gogl (the only repo with real history) is exercised only when the orchestrator remembers to run it by hand. It falsified three plan assumptions (pain constant, layer absent, corroboration inert) and caught the normalizeKind regression that 104 green fixture assertions missed. Pin a scrubbed snapshot as a checked-in fixture and assert stable properties (round-trip entry count, no unknown_type on known-real kinds).
predicted_impact: Slice B inherits a thinner corpus than its design assumes, and the next plan will again be written against documented schema rather than produced data.

title: Grep-based guards need mutation-style negative tests
detail: Two grep guards failed in different ways this feature: one pinned the defective syntax it was meant to protect (ENTRY_FIELDS bare array), and one was blind because a NUL byte made the file read as binary. Neither failure was visible. Every grep-based guard should ship a negative test that reintroduces the violation it claims to catch and asserts the guard fires. Retrofit requires auditing every existing grep guard under .bee/ — the repo has now been bitten twice (BACKLOG_STATUSES, ENTRY_FIELDS).
predicted_impact: A third grep guard silently pins the wrong thing, or silently sees nothing.

title: Iron Law RED must land as its own commit before the skill edit
detail: RED-before-GREEN ordering currently has no mechanical proof: the verify greps for headings that touch would satisfy, and RED+GREEN landed together in commit 4a1d991 so commit order proves nothing. Requiring RED as a separate, earlier commit makes ordering checkable from the commit range — cheap, and it turns a human judgment at Gate 4 into a check.
predicted_impact: A future Iron Law cell fabricates RED after the fact and nothing catches it.

title: localeCompare entry ordering is locale/ICU-dependent — byte-identical only per environment
detail: sortKey(a).localeCompare(sortKey(b)) at feedback.mjs:640-641/800-801/811 depends on the runtime locale; rankClusters tie-break already uses codepoint compare. Real corpus measured identical today (divergent index -1) so latent. Fix: one codepoint comparator across the feature, or pin a locale; touches 20+ merge assertions — its own cell. Source: review-slice-b.md P2-1.

title: Wrapper-only or guillemet-quoted titles may false-collapse to the empty cluster key
detail: normalizeTitle strips «…» to fixed point, so a legitimately guillemet-quoted title is indistinguishable from the datamark wrapper and «» normalizes toward the empty string; two distinct empty-ish titles could false-unify. Assert intended behavior for «» and quoted titles. Source: review-slice-b.md P3-5.

title: rank --json emits the datamark-stripped cluster key to the consuming agent
detail: Merge-acknowledged open P1 (Gate 4, 2026-07-10). Project key out of the CLI rank result, render representative stored wrapped title, add a test asserting key absent from rank --json. Source: review-slice-b.md P1-a.

title: normalizeTitle hand-duplicates datamark cleaning with no coupling test
detail: Merge-acknowledged open P1 (Gate 4, 2026-07-10). Export shared datamarkClean from decisions.mjs, call from both datamark and normalizeTitle, add a coupling test over datamark-relevant payloads. Source: review-slice-b.md P1-b.

title: C0 sweep is scoped to templates/*.mjs — widen to the defect class and fix the NUL in reports/evolving-9.md
detail: The sweep misses committed non-.mjs files; reports/evolving-9.md shipped with a raw NUL (git-binary, grep-invisible) two commits after the sweep landed. One cell: replace the byte with a printable escape, extend the sweep (or a docs-level check) to every committed text file, and add the .bee/bin byte-parity assertion (review P3-1/P3-3). Predicted impact: silent grep/diff blindness on any future report narrating control-byte work.

title: External reviewer dispatch lacks an AGENTS.md-bootstrap scope-out — round-1 self-block is reproducible
detail: bee-reviewing dispatches external cli reviewers with the worker prompt shape whose step 1 is Read AGENTS.md; the BEE block then demands onboarding checks a read-only reviewer cannot perform, so it self-blocks (cost: one rescue round, observed 2026-07-10). One tiny cell: add a read-only scope-out line to the reviewer dispatch instructions in bee-reviewing (SKILL.md or reviewing-reference.md) — this process is read-only, skip the bee-hive onboarding bootstrap. Predicted impact: every external review run burns a rescue round until fixed.

title: Small-lane inline reality check has no derived-constants pass — two P1s reached review in a 0-flag feature
detail: Failure analyst cross-cut: the census verified removed names, not preserved invariants (full-tree coverage, bare tokens, numeric constants derived from roster size). Candidate: add a derived-constants line to the small-lane reality check in bee-planning, or mechanize a root-scoped census helper.
impact: P1-class findings land at review instead of planning on removal/rename features

title: bee_feedback.mjs digest throws: fsutil.mjs does not export readText
detail: SyntaxError at .bee/bin/lib/feedback.mjs:29 import { readJson, readText } from ./fsutil.mjs. Note: .bee/bin/lib/fsutil.mjs and state.mjs carry pre-existing uncommitted modifications (present at session start, not from this feature) - likely an in-progress vendored-lib edit broke the export. Telemetry-only failure; close proceeded per warn-never-block.
resolved: 2026-07-11: root cause = stale 0.1.18 plugin re-vendored old helpers over committed 0.1.19; git restore to HEAD fixed it; digest green
